name: Branching Morphogenesis
description: Branching Morphogenesis in Plants/Animals. This process represents a higher-level biological mechanism distinct from molecular/biochemical processes covered in GLMP.
mermaid:
graph TD
    A12[Primary Structure] --> B12[Branching Signal]
    B12 --> C12[Pattern Formation]
    C12 --> D12[Tip Growth]
    D12 --> E12{Lateral Inhibition?}
    E12 -->|Yes| F12[Branch Suppression]
    E12 -->|No| G12[Branch Initiation]
    F12 --> H12[Pattern Maintenance]
    G12 --> I12[Secondary Branching]
    H12 --> J12[Final Architecture]
    I12 --> J12
    
    style A12 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style B12 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style C12 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000
    style D12 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style E12 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style F12 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style G12 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style H12 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style I12 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style J12 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Programming Framework: A Universal Process Visualization Methodology
sources.notes: Higher-level biological process visualization: Branching Morphogenesis. Created using the Programming Framework methodology for organismal and ecological process analysis.

sources.title: FGF10, a mesenchymally derived factor, is essential for kidney development
sources.authors: Li C, Li Q, Lu RS, Li G, Hogan BL.

notes: Higher-level biological process. GLMP covers biochemical/molecular processes; this database covers organismal/ecological processes.
